title.value: New and Stereospecific Synthesis of Allenes in a Single Step from Propargylic Alcohols

abstract.value: There are few reliable methods for the stereodefined construction of allenes, despite their growing importance in organic synthesis. As part of an earlier study, we developed a three-step procedure for the synthesis of allenes from readily available propargylic alcohols involving (1) mesylate formation, (2) displacement with excess hydrazine, and (3) oxidation with an azodicarboxylate reagent to produce a propargylic diazene intermediate that evolves dinitrogen in a sigmatropic elimination process (e.g., 1 to 3 below). Although this method did provide a stereospecific preparation of allenes, it was lengthy, unsuccessful in the preparation of the important (trimethylsilyl)-substituted allenes, and proceeded in 48-81% yield. We describe herein a new and stereospecific method for the synthesis of allenes from propargylic alcohols that proceeds in a single operation, is efficient, and provides access to a wide range of stereodefined, substituted allenes, to include (trimethylsilyl)-allenes.

warnings: No formal abstract is visible in the supplied snippets; abstract field is populated from the opening summary paragraph of this communication.

The paper uses Mitsunobu reagents and arenesulfonylhydrazine-mediated fragmentation, but no available catalyst_or_method label in the controlled vocabulary fits this noncatalytic method exactly, so 'not specified' was used.
The product scope includes a range of substituted allenes, including trimethylsilyl-substituted examples, but the title/abstract support only the broad product label 'allenes' with high confidence.

value.reaction_type: isomerization fragmentation and protonation to allenes
